add support for ollama cloud as a provider (along openrouter).

model configuration (any model) should be configured "provider:model"

like "openrouter:anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5" or "ollama:deepseek-v4"

Everything compiles. Here's a summary of what was added:

---

## Changes

**`src/provider.rs`** (new) — Provider enum and model spec parser:
- `Provider::OpenRouter | Provider::Ollama`
- `ModelSpec::parse("openrouter:anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5")` or `"ollama:deepseek-v4"` — unprefixed strings default to OpenRouter for backward compat
- `build_ollama_client(base_url, api_key)` helper

**`src/config.rs`** — Two new fields:
```json
"ollama_base_url": "http://localhost:11434",
"ollama_api_key": null
```

All model config fields (`generate_model`, `capture_model`, `inject_select_model`, `inject_compile_model`, `decompose_model`, `capture_triage_model`) now accept `provider:model` format.

**`src/generate.rs`** — Provider dispatch:
- OpenRouter → uses the new direct `openrouter::chat_once` HTTP client (richer logging with per-turn token costs + sidecars)
- Ollama → uses rig's `ollama::Client`

**`src/inject.rs`** — Provider dispatch in `wiki_navigate_and_compile` and `compile_briefing`; API key guard only fires when OpenRouter models are actually configured

**`src/capture.rs`** — Provider dispatch in `call_model_blocking` (triage) and `run_wiki_agent`; same per-model key guard pattern

**Usage examples:**
```json
{
  "generate_model": "openrouter:anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
  "inject_select_model": "ollama:qwen2.5:7b",
  "inject_compile_model": "ollama:qwen2.5:14b",
  "capture_model": "openrouter:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "ollama_base_url": "https://my-cloud-ollama.example.com"
}
```

give me a tui to configure llms to be used for the different roles with model autocompletion (i.e. pull available models and allow me to nicely select them from openrouter and/or from ollama (allow me to choose the provider if I have multiple providers configured

The command is registered. Here's what `proactive-context configure` gives you:

---

**Layout** — two-pane TUI:
- **Left** (Roles): 6 roles with their current model displayed as `[OR]/[OL] model-id`. Selected role is highlighted.
- **Right** (Models): full list from OpenRouter + Ollama, auto-fetched in a background thread while you navigate. Shows count, context length, and $/M tokens for OpenRouter models.

**Keys:**
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Tab` / `←/→` | switch between roles and models pane |
| `↑/↓` `j/k` | navigate |
| `Enter` | assign selected model to current role (auto-advances to next role) |
| `/` | open filter — type to live-filter models |
| `Esc` | close filter |
| `PgUp/PgDn` | scroll model list 10 at a time |
| `s` | save config and show toast |
| `q` / `Ctrl-C` | quit (prompts to save if unsaved changes) |

**Model sources:**
- **OpenRouter** — fetched from `api/v1/models` if `openrouter_api_key` is set. Shows as `[OR]` in cyan with `200k ctx  $0.30/M` metadata.
- **Ollama** — fetched from `<ollama_base_url>/api/tags`. Shows as `[OL]` in green. Works even without a key.

Currently assigned model gets a `✓` marker in the list.
add ollama.com cloud to my config.json as the ollama endpoint

Done. `ollama_base_url` is now set to `https://api.ollama.com`.

If you have an Ollama cloud API key, set it with:
```
proactive-context config set-key  # (currently only sets OR key)
```
or add `"ollama_api_key": "your-key"` to config.json directly, or use `proactive-context configure` and it'll be used when building the Ollama client.
Committed as `5132eed`. 13 files, +2518/-210 lines — provider abstraction, direct OpenRouter client, Ollama dispatch across all call sites, and the configure TUI.

the tui only shows me open router? how can I switch something to use ollama?

also, the role names you're using are incomprehensivlbe, I don't know what inject_select" role means...

Three things fixed:

**Role names** — now plain English:
- `inject_select` → **Pre-prompt picker** *(picks which docs are relevant before each prompt)*
- `inject_compile` → **Pre-prompt writer** *(writes the briefing injected before each prompt)*
- `capture` → **Session capture**, `capture_triage` → **Capture filter**, etc.

**Ollama cloud** — `api.ollama.com` uses `/v1/models` (OpenAI-compat format), not `/api/tags`. The fetcher now tries `/api/tags` first and falls back to `/v1/models`, handling both response shapes.

**Errors visible** — any provider fetch failure now shows as a yellow `⚠` line at the bottom of the model pane rather than disappearing silently. The title bar also shows `3×OR 12×OL` so you know what loaded.

the names are still incomprehensible "per-prompt picker" what the fuck does that mean?

improve the name but also add a label with suggestions of what type of model to use for that

Roles now show clearly what they do and what to pick:

| Role | What it does | Suggestion shown |
|------|-------------|-----------------|
| **Ask** | Answers your questions by reading your notes | → capable: sonnet, gpt-4o, llama3.3:70b |
| **Search** | Splits question into sub-searches | → fast/cheap: haiku, gpt-4o-mini, qwen2.5:7b |
| **Context scan** | Runs before EVERY prompt — scans wiki for what's relevant | → must be fast: haiku, gpt-4o-mini, qwen2.5:7b |
| **Context write** | Runs before EVERY prompt — writes the context block Claude reads | → capable: sonnet, gpt-4o, llama3.3:70b |
| **Wiki update** | After sessions — reads conversation, updates project wiki | → capable with tool-calling: sonnet, gpt-4o |
| **Skip check** | Quick yes/no: does this session have anything worth capturing? | → cheapest you have: haiku, gpt-4o-mini, qwen:3b |

The yellow suggestion line is below a divider at the bottom of the left pane when a role is selected.
what is "Ask"? where is that used?

Now when you select those roles the bottom of the pane shows:

- **`pc generate`** — `` `pc generate "question"` — answers from your notes, can read files ``
- **`pc generate (search)`** — `Splits your question into sub-searches to find more notes (part of generate)`

The other four roles (`Context scan`, `Context write`, `Wiki update`, `Skip check`) run automatically — you never invoke them manually, which is why they don't have a command name.
ok, remove the "generate" command entirely (since we now use inject as the command")

And yes — "Search" was also part of `generate`. The `decompose_model` only existed to break your question into sub-searches for the `generate` command's fan-out retrieval step. Both are gone. The configure TUI now only shows the four roles that are actually used: **Context scan**, **Context write**, **Wiki update**, **Skip check**.
what model do I use right now for retrival? I'm seeing [Image #1]

You're using **OpenRouter for embeddings** — specifically `openai/text-embedding-3-small` via `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/embeddings`. That's what's failing.

The likely cause: OpenRouter's embeddings endpoint may be returning an error (rate limit, or the model isn't available on your key). You have two options:

1. **Switch to local embeddings** (free, no API call) — set `embed_provider: "local"` and `embed_model: "all-MiniLM-L6-v2"` in `~/.proactive-context/config.json`. The model downloads once (~80MB) and runs locally via fastembed.

2. **Keep OpenRouter embeddings** — check what the actual error is by looking at the full error in the tail log.

Want to see the full error first, or just switch to local?

Done. `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` runs entirely locally via fastembed — no API calls, no key needed.

One catch: the existing index was built with OpenRouter's 1536-dim vectors. The local model produces 384-dim vectors, so the index is incompatible. You need to re-index:

```
proactive-context stop
proactive-context init
```

The `init` will restart the daemon which will re-embed everything with the local model. First run will download `all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (~80MB) automatically.

That's exactly the mismatch I mentioned. The old index has 1536-dim vectors, new embedder produces 384. Run:

```
proactive-context stop && proactive-context init
```
it should do that itself... fix it and commit

Now run `proactive-context stop && proactive-context init` once more — this time it's the last time you'll ever need to do it manually. From here on, switching embed models just requires restarting the daemon and it re-indexes itself.

Run `proactive-context init` in your own terminal (not here). The daemon gets SIGKILL'd in Claude Code's shell due to its process management. Once the daemon is up, injection will work and model switches will self-heal forever.

Committed. The fix reads `FLOAT[N]` directly from `sqlite_master` — the actual ground truth — rather than trusting `meta` which could drift. Any project DB with a stale dimension self-heals on the next non-trivial inject.
